what_it_does: This file implements a programmatic, read-only scanner for Claude Code JSONL transcripts. It makes one streaming pass over a transcript and emits per-event rows (ndjson) and a summary (json) containing event counts, structural attribution, model/token totals, boundary/segment timeline, fork provenance, message-size profiles, and inter-event gaps. It also exposes a self-session resolver that identifies the current session via explicit id, env var, or process-ancestry marker, failing loudly when ambiguous.
observations: The scanner is read-only and never mutates the source transcript.
Boundary detection is structural: a system event with subtype compact_boundary or an event with isCompactSummary==true.
Attribution is derived from event type and metadata, never from message text content.
Self-resolution walks /proc to find the ancestor claude process PID and reads a per-PID marker or fabric registration record.
It reuses build_timeline from runtime.session_pointintime so boundary/segment counts stay consistent with clone-at-point machinery.
The summary includes a dense-message profile (biggest human/assistant messages, mean bytes) and the largest time gaps as an away/sleep signal.
CLI and API are both provided: python3 -m runtime.session_scan and scan_session()/write_scan().
standouts: AmbiguousSelfError is raised rather than silently guessing self when multiple transcripts share a cwd and no unambiguous id exists.
_claude_ancestor_pid walks /proc/stat and /proc/cmdline to find the session-unique claude process.
_attribution classifies events structurally, including synthetic, compaction, channel, inject, tool, and user labels.
Fork provenance is tracked via the real forkedFrom field per event, split into inherited vs own events and parent session ids.
conventions: Follows a data-over-prose design: output is JSON/ndjson for UI projection rather than human-readable reports.
Uses leading-underscore helpers for internal functions and uppercase constants.
Docstrings include design rationale and safety arguments, not just signatures.
Reuses an existing timeline builder instead of reimplementing boundary logic.
Type hints are present on public functions.
concerns: _claude_ancestor_pid is Linux-only because it reads /proc; on other platforms it degrades to None, which can force ambiguity errors.
Nested helper _locate is redefined on every call to _self_marker.
Several files are opened with open(...) passed directly to json.load without explicit close, risking descriptor leaks on long runs.
_content_text_len falls back to json.dumps byte counts for non-string tool parts, which may overcount compared to rendered text.
Attribution still depends on a hard-coded prefix list and isMeta flag, so new injection shapes could misclassify.
os.environ.get('RECOLLECTION_CONFIG_DIR') is consulted at import time; a missing env leaves SELF_MARKER_DIR based on ~/company/.recollection.
notes: A read-only, structural transcript scanner with a strong fail-loud self-session resolver built on process ancestry and explicit session ids.
questions: What is the exact row schema defined in channel-memory/schema/session-store-grammar.md §6?
How does build_timeline classify boundaries and segments, and does it agree with this scanner on all edge cases?
What do toolUseResult events look like, and are there cases where tool-result attribution should differ?
How is the fabric registration .data/channels directory populated and kept in sync with the marker fallback?
Is _claude_ancestor_pid reliable inside containers or when /proc is restricted?
What consumer in the Company UI projects this scan output, and what fields does it actually need?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it scans a transcript into structured data without prose generation or mutation.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make session state fully observable and projectable as normalized data, while preventing the dangerous mistake of a session identifying the wrong transcript as itself.
novelty: It combines a /proc-based process-ancestry self-marker, explicit ambiguity failure, and structural attribution into a single transcript-scanning pipeline.
contribution: It contributes a deterministic, read-only transcript scanner that emits normalized per-event rows and a rich summary, plus an unambiguous self-session resolver that uses explicit ids, environment variables, and process ancestry to avoid misidentifying the current session.
summary_for_embedding: A Python module that performs a single read-only pass over a Claude Code JSONL transcript to produce per-event rows and a summary JSON with structural attribution, boundary timeline, token usage, fork provenance, and self-session resolution.
intention_for_embedding: To give the Company runtime a programmatic, data-first view of any session transcript so the UI can project session state without re-parsing prose.

symbols.does: Exception raised when self-session resolution is ambiguous and no unambiguous id is available.
symbols.description: Subclass of RuntimeError used as a teaching-loud safety gate in resolve_own_session. It prevents silently returning the newest transcript when multiple same-cwd sessions exist and no COMPANY_SESSION_ID or explicit session_id is provided.

symbols.does: Encodes a working directory into the project-dir name used under ~/.claude/projects.
symbols.description: Replaces '/' and '.' with '-' to form a filesystem-safe directory name that maps a session's cwd to its transcript storage location.

symbols.does: Walks the process tree to find the ancestor claude session process PID.
symbols.description: Reads /proc/<pid>/stat and cmdline, walking parents up to 40 steps, matching comm=='claude', basename(argv0)=='claude', or a version-string comm. Returns None on any error or no match, and is used as a session-unique key for self-marker resolution.

symbols.does: Reads the per-claude-PID self-marker to identify the current session.
symbols.description: First checks the fabric registration in .data/channels for a record with the matching claude_pid, then falls back to a JSON marker file keyed by claude ancestor PID. It validates that the recorded session_id has an existing transcript and returns session metadata, or None.

symbols.does: Finds a transcript for a session id anywhere under PROJECTS_DIR.
symbols.description: Nested helper inside _self_marker. It first checks the passed project directory, then glob-searches all project directories under ~/.claude/projects for <sid>.jsonl, returning the path and its project directory if found, otherwise (None, None).

symbols.does: Determines the transcript path for the current session unambiguously.
symbols.description: Resolution order is explicit session_id, COMPANY_SESSION_ID env, claude-PID self-marker, and finally newest-mtime only when exactly one transcript exists. It raises AmbiguousSelfError if multiple transcripts exist and no unambiguous id is available, unless allow_ambiguous=True.

symbols.does: Computes the UTF-8 byte length of rendered message content.
symbols.description: Handles string content, list-of-parts content, and tool input/result parts. For non-string values it counts the JSON-serialized bytes, returning 0 for unrecognized shapes.

symbols.does: Classifies who produced an event using structural fields only.
symbols.description: Maps event type and message metadata to labels such as assistant, synthetic, user, tool, channel, inject, compaction, or system. It avoids inferring from free-text content, using isCompactSummary, toolUseResult, isMeta, and known XML-ish prefixes.

symbols.does: Extracts token usage counts from a message's usage field.
symbols.description: Returns input, output, cache-read, and cache-creation token counts, defaulting to 0 when missing or malformed.

symbols.does: Converts an ISO 8601 timestamp string to Unix epoch seconds.
symbols.description: Replaces trailing 'Z' with '+00:00' and parses with datetime.fromisoformat. Returns None for invalid or non-string input.

symbols.does: Performs one streaming pass over a transcript to produce rows and a summary.
symbols.description: Iterates the JSONL with _iter_jsonl, reuses build_timeline for boundary/segment definitions, tallies event types, attribution, models, tokens, fork provenance, message sizes, and inter-event gaps, and returns a dict with a detailed summary and per-event row list.

symbols.does: Persists the output of scan_session to summary and optional rows files.
symbols.description: Creates the output directory, writes <stem>.summary.json and, when write_rows is True, <stem>.rows.ndjson, then returns the written paths and event count.
